conclusion_narrative: Mem0 2.0.12 silently mutates native user_id when update(metadata={'user_id': 'bob'}) is called on a memory created under user_id='alice'. Cross-tenant movement confirmed via get_all(filters=...) before and after: Alice loses visibility, Bob gains it. Reproduces Mem0 Python issue #6277.
limitations: retrospective_reconstruction from V08.1 evidence; R6 EvidenceRecord format did not exist during the original V08 execution
V08.1 was the corrective pass that produced the JSONL at evidence_commit; V08 (8871689) lacked per-tenant visibility verification but V08.1 added get_all before/after probe
harness_commit is null because V08.1 scenarios were run via validation scenario tests, not the R7 conformance harness

conclusion_narrative: Mem0 2.0.12 provides stable object identity and a history ledger natively. The version-identity sub-requirement is only partially satisfied: history-entry ids are not documented as PAMSPEC immutable version identifiers and no Mem0 operation consumes them as expected_version_id.
limitations: retrospective_reconstruction
PARTIAL analogue for PAMSPEC version identity: history-entry ids are not documented as immutable versions and no operation consumes them as expected_version_id
harness_commit is null (validation scenario test, not R7 harness)

observed_evidence.aggregate_note: native stable object identity + native history ledger; PARTIAL version identity

sub_requirements.note: Each history entry has its own UUID

Mem0 has history-entry IDs, but their equivalence to PAMSPEC immutable version identifiers is only a partial analogue. No adapter was tested; 'questionable' reflects that the requirement's applicability to Mem0's history model is uncertain rather than that an adapter supplied the behavior.

conclusion_narrative: Memory.update() has no expected_version_id parameter. Concurrent updates on the same memory object are silently accepted; last-write-wins. PAMSPEC requires an explicit version_conflict rejection for stale updates.
limitations: retrospective_reconstruction
harness_commit is null

observed_evidence.concurrency_control_kind: last-write-wins (no expected-version parameter, no conflict rejection)

conclusion_narrative: Memory.add() has no idempotency_key parameter. Repeated identical add() calls return new distinct memory IDs each time. Retry storms can create duplicate records.
limitations: retrospective_reconstruction
harness_commit is null

observed_evidence.add_signature: add(messages, user_id, agent_id, run_id, metadata, timestamp, expiration_date, infer, memory_type, prompt) - no idempotency_key

conclusion_narrative: Delete behavior has native components: DELETE recorded in history, get() returns null after deletion, stale update rejected. However, same-ID recreation is not testable (Mem0 assigns UUIDs; no public API way to request a specific ID), and a standards-grade persistent tombstone as a first-class object does not exist natively in Mem0. V08 probed the public API only; no PAMSPEC adapter was tested. Classified as gap (sub-requirements preserve the native/not_testable distinctions); adapter_feasibility=feasible.
limitations: retrospective_reconstruction
same_id_recreation is not testable through the public API (Mem0 assigns UUIDs; no way to request a specific ID)
harness_commit is null

observed_evidence.stale_update_after_delete_error: ValueError: Memory with id ... not found. Please provide a valid 'memory_id'

sub_requirements.note: history() contains a DELETE event after deletion
update() on deleted ID raises ValueError
Mem0 auto-assigns UUIDs on add(); public API exposes no way to request a specific ID. Cannot distinguish 'identity reserved against recreation' from 'random UUID happened to differ'.

No standards-grade persistent tombstone exists natively in Mem0 (the DELETE history event lacks independent lifecycle properties). No PAMSPEC adapter was tested in V08; classifying as gap rather than emulated.

conclusion_narrative: Mem0 2.0.12 stores scope tuple (user_id/agent_id/run_id) and created_at/updated_at natively. PAMSPEC-shaped provenance fields (source_actor, activity, original_source, model_ref) require the caller to write them into opaque metadata — Mem0 does not surface them natively. Mem0 records only coarse event types (ADD/UPDATE/DELETE). V08 probed the public API only; no PAMSPEC adapter was tested. Classified as gap; adapter_feasibility=feasible.
limitations: retrospective_reconstruction
Mem0 does not natively record source_actor, activity, or original_source — these must be stuffed into opaque metadata by a PAMSPEC adapter
harness_commit is null

conclusion_narrative: V08 original claim: Mem0 does not refresh the derived vector index on update(text=...). This claim is RETRACTED by the V08.1 corrective experiment. The V08 design had only one memory in the collection; a nearest-neighbor search returning that memory for both old and new queries could not distinguish stale vector from correct vector that is simply the nearest-of-one. Retained unchanged per R6 append-only protocol.

control_design.notes: Single memory in collection; search top_k=5 for old and new queries could not distinguish 'stored vector retained' from 'new vector correctly written but still nearest-of-one candidate'. Under-controlled design preserved here so the retraction is auditable.
control_design.primary_signal: hit-set membership across old-query and new-query search results

limitations: single memory in collection
no direct inspection of stored embedding
hit-set membership treated as evidence of stored-vector identity
harness_commit is null (validation scenario test, not R7 harness)
This record is RETRACTED by v08.s7.derived-state.controlled-experiment; preserved unchanged per R6 append-only protocol

conclusion_narrative: Under corrected experimental design (five distinctive control memories, maximally unrelated phrases, direct fetch of stored embedding from Chroma before and after update), Mem0 2.0.12 refreshes the derived vector. Embedding bytes changed with L2 delta ~1.374 on unit-norm vectors; old-query rank moved 0->7, new-query rank moved absent->2. Retracts v08.s7.derived-state.original-probe.

confounders_ruled_out: ranking-only inference (bypassed by direct Chroma backend inspection of stored embedding bytes)
negative_controls: five unrelated distinctive memories in the same collection so nearest-neighbor rank is meaningful
control_design.notes: Read-only inspection of Chroma backend via mem.vector_store.collection.get(ids=..., include=['embeddings']) was the primary signal. Retrieval rank/score was corroborating only.

limitations: retrospective_reconstruction

embedding-model HuggingFace snapshot revision is not pinned; a future revision could shift retrieval rank/score without invalidating the qualitative conclusion (primary evidence is the direct-embedding-bytes signal, not ranking)
evidence_source includes 'backend_inspection' which is not a Mem0 public-API probe
harness_commit is null (validation scenario test, not R7 harness)

observed_evidence.target_new_text: orbital telescope infrared spectroscopy calibration schedule
observed_evidence.target_old_text: veterinary feline dietary allergen elimination protocol

revises.reason: The V08 design used a single memory in the collection, so a nearest-neighbor search returning that memory for both old and new queries could not distinguish 'stored vector retained' from 'new vector correctly written but still nearest-of-one'. Direct fetch of the target's stored embedding from the Chroma collection was not attempted. Under a properly controlled design (five distinctive control memories plus direct fetch of stored embedding bytes via mem.vector_store.collection.get), Mem0 does refresh the vector.

conclusion_narrative: Scalar unknown extension metadata keys pass through Mem0 cleanly (native sub-requirement). Nested-dict values are rejected by Chroma at add() time with ValueError. PAMSPEC canonical_content shapes that include nested JSON would require adapter-side JSON-encoding before storing in Mem0. V08 probed the public API only; no PAMSPEC adapter was tested. Top-level classification is gap because the combined requirement does not hold natively; adapter_feasibility=feasible.
limitations: retrospective_reconstruction
emulated classification reflects that scalar keys pass through natively but nested shapes require adapter JSON-encoding — the classification describes the combination, not only what Mem0 provides
harness_commit is null

observed_evidence.nested_dict_case_add_error: ValueError: Expected metadata value to be a str, int, float, bool, SparseVector, list, or None, got {'nested': [1, 2, 3]} which is a dict

sub_requirements.note: Scalar string extension metadata keys pass through add->get->update->get cleanly
Chroma rejects nested-dict metadata values at add() time with ValueError. An adapter must JSON-encode nested shapes before storing.
